channel.title: Adrian Lissot's Blog
channel.description: Recent content on Adrian Lissot's Blog

item.title: Github GameOff 2021 - Grand Matrix Reloaded
item.description: Check out my entry to Github GameOff 2021, Grand Matrix Reloaded! Credits: Adrian Lissot, Barthélémy Renucci, Emily Lissot (my sister!).

item.title: js13k-2021 - Space Cowboy
item.description: Check out my entry to js13k 2021, Space Cowboy! Credits: Adrian Lissot, Barthélémy Renucci, Florent Perez.

item.title: MakeCode Arcade - Lost Viking
item.description: Check out my game made using Microsoft MakeCode Arcade, Lost Viking! There’s also a detailed post in the MakeCode’s forum: Forum Post Credits: Adrian Lissot.

item.title: Js13k 2020 post-mortem 2/3
item.description: Js13k 2020 post-mortem 2/3 This article is the second part of a three part series of the js13k post-mortem. This time, I’ll show you how we’ve integrated visual assets into the game! Thanks to the experience I had from last year’s jam, I quickly turned into SVGs for all visual assets, for multiple reasons: SVGs are lightweight SVGs compress very well SVGs can be displayed in the browser natively There are plenty of great SVG editors (Inkscape, Illustrator) Bonus: we used SVGs draw all elements' colliders!

item.title: Js13k 2020 post-mortem 1/3
item.description: Js13k 2020 post-mortem 1/3 A quick reminder of what the js13k is: Js13kGames is a JavaScript coding competition for HTML5 Game Developers. The fun part of the compo is the file size limit set to 13 kilobytes. The competition started at 13:00 CEST, 13th August and ended at 13:00 CEST, 13th September 2020. Theme for this year was 404. This year, unlike 2019 where I worked on my own, I brought some friends to the competition!

item.title: js13k-2020 - 404 BC Pinball
item.description: Check out my entry to js13k 2020, 404 BC Pinball! Credits: Adrian Lissot, Barthélémy Renucci, David Sebaoun, Florent Perez.

item.title: js13k-2019 - Playback
item.description: Check out my entry to js13k 2019, Playback! Credits: Adrian Lissot.

I’m Adrian Lissot, a software engineer and hobbyist game dev living in Dublin, Ireland. I currently work for Microsoft. I previously worked SoftFluent, Ubisoft, Marquardt and IBM. I work with .NET, C#, React and TypeScript in my day-to-day job and sometimes participate in game jams using JavaScript and Godot.
